reasoning: `implies_apply` is external_body bridging `p.implies(q).satisfied_by(ex)` — which evaluates a fresh closure from `TempPred::new` — to the direct modus ponens conclusion. This is an unverified trust assumption resolving the intensional closure gap.
This is literally the statement of `entails_apply` itself — a correct and desirable modus ponens for entailment. No new trust surface beyond φ1.
If `p` holds on all executions (`valid(p)`), then any spec trivially entails `p` — this is the definition of validity. A valid property being entailed by everything is correct and expected.
An unsatisfiable spec vacuously entailing anything is standard ex falso quodlibet — a fundamental property of implication, not a spec gap.
Transitivity of entailment is a standard and desirable logical property. It composes `entails_apply` correctly with no new trust surface beyond φ1.
